title: Product vision brief: starter strategy demo
durable_vision: The ProductOS starter should show a reviewable, traceable product strategy and discovery chain before a new team replaces the demo data with its own reality.
near_term_product_goal: Help a new team understand why ProductOS exists and how one coherent product bet flows into discovery and delivery artifacts.
problem_space_summary: Blank templates do not show how ProductOS should think, decide, and preserve context across a product workflow.
solution_direction_summary: Seed the starter with a compact but explicit strategy spine that connects mission, vision, market posture, and problem framing.
customer_value_statement: New teams can inspect one coherent strategy and discovery chain instead of guessing what ProductOS expects them to produce.
business_value_statement: A strategy-aware starter should improve adoption confidence and reduce the setup friction that makes ProductOS feel too abstract.
differentiation_statement: The starter demonstrates ProductOS as a disciplined operating model, not a generic template pack.
north_star_definition: Time required for a new team to explain the seeded mission, product vision, and downstream problem framing accurately.

input_guardrail_metrics.definition: The seeded starter keeps strategy refs visible from mission through problem framing.

next_bets: Keep the strategy spine readable before expanding seeded workflow breadth.

evidence_refs.justification: The starter strategy context explains the adoption bet and constraints.
